enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer request from a teenager named Andrew to make a video about being a teenager. He offers advice on building a life trajectory early, values knowledge over wealth, contrasts his own generation's technological revolution with what he sees as an upcoming cultural/mental revolution for today's teens, and touches on education reform, self-motivation, and health.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Success should be measured by knowledge and ideas, not money or possessions
Teens should identify their trajectory early and treat every action as building toward a long-term goal
Schools should provide a clear, government-sponsored open knowledge map rather than expensive proprietary curricula
People will increasingly self-educate online, making traditional college unnecessary
His generation experienced the technological revolution; today's teens will experience a cultural/mental revolution
People should share trade secrets freely to advance society rather than hoarding them out of fear
Homeless people should be judged by their ideas and knowledge, not their possessions
Fear-mongering about war and the environment is overblown; the future is bright for today's youth
Teens should protect their bodies from injury early because damage compounds over time

transcript.text: Hey, here's Tired and Stoned video request from the youngster.

He wants me to do a video called Being a Teenager.
Okay, so Being a Teenager.
So teens are the people between the ages of 13 and 19.
That's six years.
So to somebody like me, six years is kind of like a project.
You know, it's like you're kind of the same person, you know.
It's like whatever.
It's like a couple weeks go by.
But to a teenager, that's the transition from child to adult.
And it's like seems like a really long time ago and so much happens and lots of change and lots of learning and stuff.
Yeah, and so I guess it's kind of figuring out like what you're all about.
So I guess I would do this video and make it kind of like an advice.
Like maybe like advice to the younger me, to the teen me or advice to teens or my perspective on what I do.

Like when you're just leaving high school and leaving home.
That's like when you're first starting to take responsibility for your own self and your own trajectory in life.
And so I think any successful person would tell you that they started their trajectory early.
They knew what they wanted to do or everything that they did ended up lining up to help them become who they are.
